title: AI System Design: From Idea to Production - Apoorva Joshi, MongoDB
summary: Apoorva Joshi presents a structured framework for designing AI systems from idea to production, arguing that identifying the business problem, defining success metrics, and selecting appropriate architecture are more critical than coding ability. The talk addresses why most AI systems fail to reach production due to poor early-stage decisions and demonstrates the framework applied to real-world problems.
writeup: Apoorva Joshi of MongoDB lays out a structured framework for taking AI systems from idea to production, treating problem definition and architecture choice as more decisive than coding skill. The talk attributes most failures to reach production to poor early-stage decisions: skipping a clear business problem, undefined success metrics, and mismatched architecture. Concrete takeaways include identifying the business problem first, defining success metrics up front, and selecting an appropriate cognitive architecture, with model evaluation as part of the process. The framework is demonstrated against real-world problems.
